title: drosera paleacea subsp. stelliflora
intro: Drosera paleacea subsp. stelliflora is a subspecies of the Drosera paleacea plant, commonly known as the pale sundew, a carnivorous plant native to Australia. It has medicinal properties due to its high content of iridoid glycosides, particularly in its leaves, roots, and rhizomes. The health benefits of Drosera paleacea subsp. stelliflora include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. Drosera paleacea subsp. stelliflora can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, tincture, or decoction, typically using the roots, leaves, or aerial parts in combination with other herbs, but it's essential to consult a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
